name: Margaret Croke
bio: Margaret Croke is an Illinois State Representative (12th District, Chicago — Lakeview East, Lincoln Park, Old Town, Gold Coast) since 2021. She's running for Illinois Comptroller to succeed retiring incumbent Susana Mendoza. Croke was Deputy Chief of Staff at the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity before her election. She chairs the Financial Institutions Committee and serves on the House Revenue & Finance Committee. Born in Oak Park, raised in Chicago. University of Michigan grad (Political Science + Communications). Endorsed by Gov. JB Pritzker and Cook County Democratic Party — the frontrunner in the 4-way primary.

jacket.note: $425K raised (Oct 2025 figure), $510K cash on hand. Top fundraiser in the race. Critics note some donations come from super PACs and Republican donors.

donors.name: Cook County Democratic Party
donors.label: Cook County Dem Party endorsement + support

red_flags.label: Super PAC / Republican Donor Contributions
red_flags.detail: Opponent Karina Villa criticized Croke's donations as including super PAC money and contributions from Republican donors. Croke leads all fundraising in the race with $425K raised.

key_votes.summary: Chairs the House Financial Institutions Committee — the body that oversees banking, depositories, and payment processing in Illinois. Croke argues this makes her uniquely qualified for the Comptroller role.
